rows: Avalon Hotel and Bungalows Palm Springs

Called the front desk multiple times no answer. When someone did answer, was transferred to “reservations” (?) is this just a teenage guy in his bedroom? Spoke zero English, couldn’t understand him, line was breaking up and then he hung up 🤣 I was about to book a 4 night stay in the best room too! No thanks. Will go to  Fairmont!

Dear Ms. Jasmin,     Thank you for your feedback, and I’m very sorry for the frustration you experienced. This is not the level of service we strive to provide, and we are reviewing what happened with our phone and reservations handling.    We would truly love the opportunity to welcome you back and provide a proper experience. If you decide to consider us again, please feel free to reach out directly via email — we would be happy to personally ensure you are well taken care of.    Warm Regards,    Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper

Beyond frustrated with this property. From start to finish, the experience has been baffling.    I was placed in a room that somehow managed to face a wall and be partially on open to a hallway—dark, awkward, and completely unsuitable.    The real issue, however, is the billing incompetence. At check-in, I explained that I was having a physical issue with my business credit card that was used to book (chip malfunction). So instead of making the front desk type in my corporate card number, I presented my personal card with the clear expectation—standard practice at any reputable hotel—that it would be used for incidentals only, while the booking card would be charged for the stay.    Instead, without explanation or warning, the hotel charged the entire stay to my personal card.    Since then, multiple staff members on the Proper side have chosen to act deliberately obtuse and inflexible, refusing to simply move the charge to the card used for booking. This is not a complicated request, nor is it unusual—yet the team has taken an oddly forceful, almost adversarial stance rather than resolving a straightforward billing correction.    This is a classic skin deep level luxury property. This is such an easily solved issue, you have the card on file, just charge it instead of making me email multiple times and dispute with Amex

Dear Mr. Bailey,     Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. I’m truly sorry for the frustration this situation has caused.    I’d like to provide some clarity around our billing procedures. For security purposes, it is hotel policy that the credit card presented at check-in must be physically present and is the card authorized for charges unless a Credit Card Authorization form is completed in advance. If a different card is to be used for payment, a completed authorization form is required at least 24 hours prior to arrival (and typically 48 hours in advance) to allow for proper verification.    Because the corporate card was not physically present at check-in and no prior authorization form had been submitted, our team was required to proceed with the personal card provided.     We truly would have liked for you to have had a seamless experience and to fully enjoy your time at our property. I’m sorry that this situation impacted your stay, and I appreciate you sharing your concerns with us. If you choose to give us another opportunity in the future, please feel free to reach out to me directly—I would be happy to personally assist with your arrangements.    Warm regards,     Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper

If you're looking for a view stay out of room 243. Im shocked being and amex platinum member booking though amex travel I recieved this room. I commented to amex travel who said the front desk would take care of it. Their solution was to charge an additional $400 per night. Did I mention the late night work in the back alley. So far not a pleasurable stay

Absolutely horrible service, they don't care. It's a shame because it's a beautiful property so close to the Santa Monica beach. At check-in, the front desk is so impatient and doesn't even bother telling us where our room is, and when we asked, she told us to ask someone else. The hostess at every restaurant has been rude. There's never a table available for breakfast no matter what time we try from 7 to 10 am throughout our 3 night stay. Instead of offering alternatives like to go, in room dining, the hostess will just say there's no table. But I clearly see open tables, nobody bothers to clean them. I understand if they are busy, but this problem can be easily solved through a reservation system, and training the hostess to be more empathetic. They closed the main restaurant due to weather, which is also understandable, but there's 0 signage on where the new location is, and every person I asked was extremely arrogant and didn't seem to care.    The room is tiny and although it looks aesthetic, they use the cheapest material for everything, even the toilet paper. The pillows are too soft and the windows are too thin. It's  loud here at night, as you can hear every car and person talking downstairs.    Very disappointed in the service and management. I really wish to come back because the building is historic and gorgeous.

Dear Mr. Tony Pan,     Thank you for your feedback, and we’re truly sorry that your experience did not reflect the level of service we are known for. This is certainly not typical of our team, and we sincerely regret the disappointment it caused. We have addressed your comments directly with our teams, and we genuinely appreciate you taking the time to share them, as feedback like this helps us improve and do better.     We hope to welcome you back for a Proper experience—should you decide to give us a second chance, please feel free to reach out directly, we would be happy to personally ensure you’re able to enjoy our restaurants and all of the outlets that make our property truly special.    Warm Regards,     Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper
We cancelled an event 18 months in advance and they still would not give us our $5,000 deposit back. Robbery.

We stayed at Santa Monica Proper expecting calm, design, and actual hospitality. What we got was one of the worst hotel experiences I have had in years.    Room 270 is in the so called “wellness wing.” The irony is brutal. The door is paper thin and directly outside is the wellness lounge. Housekeeping and staff were in and out nonstop, with doors that slam like a bank vault closing. This was not occasional. It was every 5 to 10 minutes, including between 11pm and 6am. Add staff walkie talkies bleeding straight through the door and you have a room that is functionally uninhabitable if you value sleep. I slept about two hours.    At 6am I politely asked for a room change. They promised a Grand King, which is only about $20 more than the Performance King. Fine. I did not ask for an upgrade. I asked for basic peace and quiet.    Then the room itself piled on. The bathroom window was completely off its track, leaving the bathroom like a cold plunge. It felt around 45F all night and into the morning. “Wellness” apparently means hypothermia plus noise.    Joanna at the front desk told us we could move after breakfast. When I returned, the story changed. Suddenly we could not move until much later. I called throughout the afternoon. No urgency, no proactive update, no priority given to a guest who did not sleep. We finally got access to the new room at 5pm. That meant we spent the entire day stuck in the loud, freezing room, unable to unpack because we were told we had to move. Wasted day.

We stayed at Santa Monica Proper expecting calm, design, and actual hospitality. What we got was one of the worst hotel experiences I have had in years.    Room 270 is in the so called “wellness wing.” The irony is brutal. The door is paper thin and directly outside is the wellness lounge. Housekeeping and staff were in and out nonstop, with doors that slam like a bank vault closing. This was not occasional. It was every 5 to 10 minutes, including between 11pm and 6am. Add staff walkie talkies bleeding straight through the door and you have a room that is functionally uninhabitable if you value sleep. I slept about two hours.    At 6am I politely asked for a room change. They promised a Grand King, which is only about $20 more than the Performance King. Fine. I did not ask for an upgrade. I asked for basic peace and quiet.    Then the room itself piled on. The bathroom window was completely off its track, leaving the bathroom like a cold plunge. It felt around 45F all night and into the morning. “Wellness” apparently means hypothermia plus noise.    Joanna at the front desk told us we could move after breakfast. When I returned, the story changed. Suddenly we could not move until much later. I called throughout the afternoon. No urgency, no proactive update, no priority given to a guest who did not sleep. We finally got access to the new room at 5pm. That meant we spent the entire day stuck in the loud, freezing room, unable to unpack because we were told we had to move. Wasted day. Zero ownership.    They also claimed the new room was a “Jr Suite.” It was not. It was just the Grand King. Mislabeling a basic room swap as a suite is not service. It is spin.    The hotel felt severely understaffed and utterly indifferent. The design in the photos and the wellness theme pulled us in, but in person the design feels dated and tired. The “wellness wing” feels like an afterthought tacked on to chase the Santa Monica vibe.    Do not stay here if you want rest, quiet, or competent guest service. This was 1 star service wrapped in luxury marketing. Management should contact me directly because this was unacceptable and needs to be made right.

Dear Mr. Ladendorf,     Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. I am truly sorry to hear about your experience at Santa Monica Proper. What you described is highly unusual and certainly not the level of comfort, service, or hospitality we are known for.    Please accept my sincere apologies for the noise disturbances, the condition of the room, and the lack of urgency and communication surrounding your room move. A restful, seamless stay is fundamental to our promise, and it is clear that we fell short in several critical areas. I also regret the confusion around the room type and the impact this situation had on your day and overall experience.    We have waived the late checkout fee of $200. While this does not undo the frustration you experienced, we hope it reflects our acknowledgment of the inconvenience caused.    We would appreciate the opportunity to welcome you back and provide you with the true Proper experience. Should you choose to return, I would be happy to oversee your reservation to ensure your stay is comfortable, quiet, and restorative from start to finish.    Thank you again for bringing this to our attention. Your feedback is invaluable, and it allows us to address these issues directly and improve moving forward. Please feel free to contact me directly should you wish to discuss this further.    Warm regards,    Armando Campos  Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper
CHECK OUT OTHER HOTELS IN AREA
Negotiating the rate after the room was booked to try to get more money. Went from $200 to $150 upsale after 65000 pts   Not good practice.   Be careful!!!

Dear Mr. Montalvo,     Thank you for sharing your feedback. I’m truly sorry for the inconvenience and frustration caused by your experience with the room upgrade process. This is not the impression we aim to leave with our guests.    Please accept our apologies if the communication around the upgrade felt unclear or uncomfortable. We value transparency and want every interaction to feel respectful and straightforward.    We would truly appreciate the opportunity to welcome you back and provide you with the Proper experience that reflects our standards. Should you choose to return, I would be happy to oversee your reservation to ensure your stay is seamless and memorable.    Warm regards,    Armando Campos  Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper

En repetidas ocasiones pedimos que arreglara la caja fuerte que no servía y nunca asistieron igual reportamos una de las ventanas que no cerraba y no asistieron,  la habitación limpia, pero ya un poco pasada de época, no estaba como la foto, muy ruidosa, se escuchaba todo el tránsito de autos y gente de la calle, el lobby muy bonito y el restaurante también, sin embargo las habitaciones nos quedaron a deber! Al final nos hicieron un descuento por lo de la caja fuerte.  Gracias

Estimada Patricia ,    Gracias por compartir sus comentarios sobre su reciente estancia en el hotel.     Agradecemos su opinión y estamos comprometidos con la mejora continua. Gracias por compartir las areas específicas donde podríamos mejorar.   Su satisfacción es nuestra prioridad y esperamos poder atender cualquier inquietud para garantizar una mejor experiencia en su próxima estancia.    Atentamente,  Felipe Reyes Deeney  Director of Guest Experience & Guest Services
For the price and all the hype it is very underwhelming. rooms are very average, one can say below average. And the backside of the hotel is constantly noisy from trucks and garbage pick up.

Dear Mohammad,     Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry you experienced noise disturbance during your stay and that the room didn’t fully meet your expectations. We’d love the opportunity to better accommodate you on a future visit with a quieter room location and a more comfortable setup.    Please feel free to reach out to us directly should you decide to give us another chance.    Warm regards,    Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper

Good: Receptionist are very friendly, understanding & instant problem-solvers. Well done.    Superb Gym.    Bad: We got a room where we could hear the service door next to our room every few minutes, for 500 bucks a night?!         Plastic toilet seats with no softclosing lids. Just one shower outlet, very difficult to shower without washing hair.         Price performance foodwise disappoints.         Sauna/Cold Plunch is not included in the room fee, very unusual for a 5 star hotel. 150 dollar to rent for 1 person and extra 50 bucks to add another person. What is this?        Crossing paths when you step out of the elevator at ground floor and walking either to the lobby or gym. You really have to watch out to not bump into somebody. The staff is always in a rush and the corridors are very narrow.

It really is a beautiful hotel and has great amenities specifically within wellness. The cleaning lady Lulu was so accommodating and always checked in on us while we were there. The wellness room was infront of the wellness club but there’s no sign so we didn’t know it was there. We booked a private session for the red light therapy but no one showed up for about 30 minutes we ended up cancelling. The room was next to housekeeping so there was constant noise throughout the whole night. Double check on your rooms location. The service could’ve been a lot better but overall we loved the concept it just wasn’t executed for the wellness hotel room.

Dear Ms. Margalef,    Thank you for your feedback. We’re truly sorry for the challenges you experienced, including the red light therapy appointment, room location, and noise. Your comments are incredibly valuable, and we have addressed these concerns with our team to help ensure they do not happen again. We are continuously working to enhance the guest experience and genuinely appreciate your input. We hope you’ll give us another chance to welcome you back for a more seamless and enjoyable stay.    Warm Regards,    Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper

Beautiful hotel — if you only care about how things look on Instagram.    My first room had absolutely no sunlight. At 2 PM, it still felt like living in a designer cave. We decided to pay extra to upgrade to a “Deluxe Suite,” which, spoiler alert, turned out to be just a slightly bigger king room with sunlight as the only upgrade.    No bathtub (you can pay extra to use their spa pods though 🤡), one tiny sink, a slow old toilet, and a mysterious drain smell that made me rethink basic hygiene.    The room’s soundproofing was so bad we got a free sample of the city nightlife every night.    Service? The front desk was genuinely polite. Everyone else seemed... elsewhere. The bellmen were more invested in their own conversations than in luggage, and some random person with a notebook knocked on our door to “check” when we were leaving — despite our confirmed 4 PM late checkout. No apology, no intro, just vibes.    This hotel is gorgeous in photos but exhausting in real life. A+ for aesthetics, D- for actually being a place to stay.

Dear Ailee,     Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. While we’re pleased to know you appreciated the design of the hotel, we’re truly sorry that your overall experience did not meet expectations. The comfort and satisfaction of our guests are our top priorities, and we regret that several aspects of your stay fell short.    Please know that your comments have been carefully reviewed with our department leaders, as this is not the level of service we strive to provide—nor is it typical of our guest experience. We are continuously working to enhance our operations and ensure every stay is exceptional. Your feedback is truly valued and helps us continue to improve.    We hope you’ll give us another opportunity to welcome you back for a Proper experience.     Warm regards,     Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper
I ordered a pizza and a salad at their restaurant. That’s it. No drinks. $75 bill. Completely overpriced. No business should charge this much money.
The WiFi was poor or not working. In n out of service. Making it difficult to work.     The shower was dripping so badly it kept us up. And it was not possible to shut it off entirely.

Dear Charlie,    We’re sorry to see your 2/5 star rating. We would truly appreciate any feedback you’re willing to share so we can better understand your experience and address any concerns. We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back in the future for a more seamless and enjoyable stay.    Warm regards,     Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper
Went there for a wedding reception. Food was not great.Not crazy about location.
Not close to 5 star hotel ! Property 4 star, service 3 star!

Dear Jasper,     Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that your experience didn’t meet your expectations, as this is not the kind of comment we typically receive. Our team strives to provide the highest level of service and comfort to every guest, and we’ll be reviewing your remarks to ensure we continue to improve. We hope you’ll give us another opportunity to welcome you back for a much better experience.    Warm regards,    Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper
Small room, bad layout and a lot of noise from the roof top bar - btw the rooftop and pool were closed due do a private arrangement

Dear Jan,    We’re sorry to hear that you found your room small and the layout less than ideal, as well as experiencing noise from the rooftop bar. It’s uncommon for our rooftop to be closed, but when it is for private events, we always strive to offer our guests the best alternative options to enjoy the area.    We truly hope to welcome you back for a more comfortable and enjoyable experience.    Warm regards,     Armando Campos   Managing Director   Santa Monica Proper
Was planning to do a staycation at this hotel and cancelled once I found out that the parking fee is $75/ night. Absolutely outrageous! Choosing another hotel with a reasonable parking rate.

Dear Dionnel,     Thank you for your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that our parking fee affected your plans. If you ever change your mind and decide to give us a chance, we’d be delighted to welcome you.     Warm Regards,     Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper

I appreciate that this hotel is pet-friendly, but I think it’s important for management to also consider guests with pet allergies. Not everyone cleans their pets the same, and allergens can linger even after cleaning. It would be great if hotels offered designated pet-free or hypoallergenic rooms so guests with sensitivities can also enjoy their stay without discomfort. This would make the hotel more inclusive and comfortable for everyone.  Also carpeting and rugs shouldn't be in rooms either Pets, they have dander in them and what if an animal urinated on it?!

Dear Guest,    Thank you very much for sharing your thoughtful feedback. We truly appreciate your perspective on accommodating guests with pet allergies. Your suggestions about designated pet-free or hypoallergenic rooms, as well as reconsidering the use of carpets and rugs, are very valuable to us.    We want to assure you that all of our rooms are thoroughly cleaned and carefully inspected multiple times before each new guest checks in, to ensure the highest standards of cleanliness and comfort.    We will review your comments with our management team as we continue to make our hotel as inclusive and comfortable as possible.    Warm Regards,     Armando Campos   Managing Director  Santa Monica Proper

I came in this evening on a quiet Sunday to grab a drink at the lobby bar with my boyfriend. However, my evening came to an end when I was harassed and followed by a man who claimed he was a manager named Jonathan. I later found out he was security on duty. The hotel was empty, except for a few people having drinks and one man lingering the hotel off the street, who eventually walked up to me and made me feel uncomfortable. Instead of the hotel, focusing on this random man, a blonde woman from the bar, walked up to me and said I could not take pictures where I was standing. I was off to a corner with no one in sight, she asked me to tell the reception that I was taking pictures and for them to give me permission. Before I could even walk over there. The security named Jonathan came at me very close to my face and demanded that I leave the property. I asked what I did because I’ve only been walking around the common area for a couple minutes and he said that I can’t take pictures. I let him know that on social media many people take pictures in this hotel and I don’t understand why I am being targeted. I’m not sure if this is a form of racism, but I want to write this review to let females know, especially younger women that the men in this hotel are extremely creepy, I felt like I was going to get touched at any moment. I left out the door which led me outside, and he continued to follow me walking very close to me. I ended up taking a picture of him and asked him what his name was. He refused to give me his name so I asked him to then call the police because I don’t see what I did wrong as A paying customer, only I didn’t even have a chance to sit in order a drink. I typically frequent the Waldorf, Beverly Hills, Hotel, and other high-end hotels where I like to have a drink and admire the beautiful surroundings. It’s unfortunate that it’s been five years since I stepped into this establishment and within seconds, I get harassed like this. I hope that management will see this and take the matter seriously. I will be writing reviews everywhere I can and posting on my social media where I have over 1 million followers. It’s amazing to me that being in a city full of influencers and people who do social media I walked in with my iPhone and tiny point-and-shoot camera and was treated as if I walked in with a big media crew full of professional cameras, lights, and microphones, this place really needs to lighten up and if it’s that big of a problem, maybe they should check their social media and reach out to everyone. Who’s ever posted a picture in there lobby and common area and get on them as well.
Shutters on the Beach Santa Monica, CA
Awesome hotel. Great vibe. Great people. Great room. Great food. From check  in to leaving - the experience was awesome.
Beautiful hotel and rooms! We enjoyed both restaurants/bars and tried a variety of cocktails and food. We had great service as well.

10/10 stay. Santa Monica Proper completely won me over.    Even before I arrived, Armando, the MD, reached out and was helpful and thoughtful. A really nice touch. My check-in experience deserves a callout. The front desk team was warm, efficient, and incredibly helpful.    Service throughout the hotel was consistently excellent. The pool bar servers were wonderful, and I have to mention the chicory salad I ordered. It was so good I ordered it two days in a row. Craving it now!    The gym was very well done, not a sad little hotel gym. I also loved the small touches, like the house made granola bars. So simple, but such a nice add.    It goes without saying that the Kelly Wearstler design is gorgeous, but what impressed me most was how livable the space feels. Cozy corners everywhere, thoughtful details, and a vibe that feels elevated without being precious.    What stood out most was the consistency. It wasn’t just one great interaction. Everyone I encountered, from the front desk to the restaurant team, clearly cared and went out of their way to be helpful.    Beautiful design, great energy, and truly exceptional hospitality. I would absolutely stay here again.

Beautiful hotel with an incredibly helpful and friendly staff - did an engagement party here and everyone went above and beyond, despite an issue with the rooftop restaurant being rained out. They took care of us.

Nous avons passé 15 jours dans cet merveilleux hôtel . Déjà l’hôtel est magnifique. Les chambres sont spacieuses et confortable .   Le petit déjeuner était mon moment préféré de la journée, la carte est très variée et plein d’options saine et healthy . Le personnel est incroyablement chaleureux.     L’hôtel est très bien placé, et tous est à distance de marche : plage , magasin, bar , restaurant.       Si je dois critiquer quelque chose , ça serrai la réactivité de certaines demande , mais nous y étions pendant les fêtes de fin d’années , donc je pense que c’est liée à cela .     J’y reviendrai 🤍✨.     Le personnels est incroyablement gentil et chaleureux ( serveur, front desk, valet )

We had a wonderful stay at the Santa Monica Proper Hotel. Everything about the experience felt thoughtful and elevated, from the design to the service. The hotel is beautiful, stylish, and incredibly comfortable.    The food was exceptional—truly a standout. Every meal was fresh, creative, and memorable, easily among the best hotel dining we’ve experienced.    Overall, a fantastic stay and a place we’d absolutely return to—even if just for the food. Highly recommended if you’re staying in Santa Monica and want something both luxurious and welcoming, with easy walking access to attractions like the Santa Monica Pier.

Beautifully decorated hotel and lovely rooftop pool area.     Sadly we weren’t able to enjoy it whatsoever due to it being closed for rain the entire time we were there. Disappointing that the hotel did not eventually open the rooftop bar area even though it’s covered. We felt like we lost out on the main amenity that the resort fees were charged for.

Absolutely horrible service, they don't care. It's a shame because it's a beautiful property so close to the Santa Monica beach. At check-in, the front desk is so impatient and doesn't even bother telling us where our room is, and when we asked, she told us to ask someone else. The hostess at every restaurant has been rude. There's never a table available for breakfast no matter what time we try from 7 to 10 am throughout our 3 night stay. Instead of offering alternatives like to go, in room dining, the hostess will just say there's no table. But I clearly see open tables, nobody bothers to clean them. I understand if they are busy, but this problem can be easily solved through a reservation system, and training the hostess to be more empathetic. They closed the main restaurant due to weather, which is also understandable, but there's 0 signage on where the new location is, and every person I asked was extremely arrogant and didn't seem to care.    The room is tiny and although it looks aesthetic, they use the cheapest material for everything, even the toilet paper. The pillows are too soft and the windows are too thin. It's  loud here at night, as you can hear every car and person talking downstairs.    Very disappointed in the service and management. I really wish to come back because the building is historic and gorgeous.

This hotel is absolutely charming and elegant. We thoroughly enjoyed the rooftop bar and restaurant, as well as the pool area. The aesthetics and overall ambiance are exceptional, with beautifully repeated circular arches throughout the property. The restaurant’s atmosphere is especially inviting, and we loved relaxing on the large collection of comfortable sofas and chairs. The hotel is truly unique, and its design perfectly reflects that individuality. The décor and customer service were outstanding—every staff member was warm, accommodating, and pleasant. We will most definitely stay here again. We highly recommend this hotel.

We stayed at The Proper Hotel Santa Monica with our family and had the best trip. The hotel is beautiful, spotless, and thoughtfully designed in a way that feels calm and elevated.   The food was excellent—fresh, healthy, and actually made us feel good. One of the biggest highlights for us was the scent-free environment. No overpowering fragrances anywhere, which meant we slept incredibly well (such a game changer, especially with kids).  Every detail felt intentional, from cleanliness to comfort. It’s rare to find a hotel that feels this good for both adults and families. We’ll absolutely be back.

Staff, room service, both lobby and rooftop restaurants.

En repetidas ocasiones pedimos que arreglara la caja fuerte que no servía y nunca asistieron igual reportamos una de las ventanas que no cerraba y no asistieron,  la habitación limpia, pero ya un poco pasada de época, no estaba como la foto, muy ruidosa, se escuchaba todo el tránsito de autos y gente de la calle, el lobby muy bonito y el restaurante también, sin embargo las habitaciones nos quedaron a deber! Al final nos hicieron un descuento por lo de la caja fuerte.  Gracias

The Santa Monica Proper Hotel is not only place to stay, it's amasterclass in modern and bohemian luxury, immediately establishing itself as the premier design hotel in Santa Monica. From the moment you step into the lobby, designed by the brilliant Kelly Wearstler, you are immersed in a world of tactile textures, vintage pieces, and organic tones that exude effortless West Coast sophistication.    Design and Atmosphere  The interior spaces are artfully composed, feeling simultaneously residential and grand. The high ceilings, subtle lighting, and curated art pieces create a warm, inviting atmosphere that encourages lingering. Unlike many sterile luxury hotels, the Proper feels alive and inspired. It’s the perfect blend of coastal chill and high design.    The Rooftop Experience  The centerpiece of the hotel is undoubtedly the stunning Rooftop Pool and Calabra Restaurant. Offering breathtaking panoramic views of the Pacific Ocean and Santa Monica cityscape, this area is a pure coastal oasis. Dining here, especially at sunset, is a mandatory part of the experience, with Mediterranean-inspired cuisine and exceptional cocktails. It truly captures the essence of Southern California luxury.    Location and Service  The hotel’s location is ideal, placing guests within easy walking distance of the beach, the 3rd Street Promenade, and the historic Santa Monica Pier. Beyond the stunning aesthetics, the service is polished and genuinely attentive, reflecting a high standard of hospitality. Staff are knowledgeable and anticipate needs without being intrusive.    Conclusion:  For travelers seeking a sophisticated, unique, and truly luxurious experience in Santa Monica, the Proper sets an entirely new benchmark. It is a destination in itself and an absolute five-star recommendation for a memorable California stay. just make sure have enough budget before staying at this hotel, since the room price to stay here can be different
Super nice and kind staff! The food was excellent! The hotel is gorgeous in my opinion and our room was so clean and spacious! We have enjoyed our stay and will definitely coming back!
Good: Designerski hotel. Bardzo miła obsługa. Czysto. Kuchnia bardzo dobra. Kawa espresso przygotowana perfekt,co w przypadku hoteli jest wyjątkowe.

This is my third time staying at the Santa Monica Proper Hotel and, as usual, they didn’t disappoint. The rooms are beautifully designed  (with extremely comfortable beds), the service is impeccable, and while the hotel is both luxurious and classy, they have somehow managed to make it quite «homey» and cosy.    The interior designers deserve a medal, and also a thank you from me, as their beautiful choices have inspired some of my own home designs .   When I stay at the Santa Monica Proper, I don’t feel like leaving the Proper-ty at all (pun intended;) and honestly - I don’t have to!  From having breakfast and chilling by the pool in the morning, bringing my pc to the lounge bar, joining what almost feels like a social hub in the daytime, to having sunset aperitivo at the rooftop bar and dinner at La Calabra or Palma (both with excellent food and atmosphere) Santa Monica Proper is indeed a destination in itself.   Thank you for another great stay. I can’t wait to come back!

The Proper feels like someone took the very best parts of coastal luxury and stripped out every ounce of pretension, leaving behind a space that’s effortlessly cool. You walk in and the design hits you first, Kelly Wearstler textures, sun-washed tones, curves and stone that make the whole place feel like living inside a modern California daydream.    The rooftop is the real magnet. Ocean breeze, smart cocktails, food that actually tastes as good as it looks, and a view that makes you forget your to-do list exists. The rooms are calm and intentional, more sanctuary than hotel, with that clean beach-city energy that resets your nervous system the second you drop your bags. Everything here feels curated but never stiff, luxurious but never loud. It’s Santa Monica elegance with a pulse. A place that makes you feel like you checked in to take your life up a level, not just sleep in a nicer bed.

We stayed at The Proper for 3 weeks when we were evacuated from our home during the Palisades fires.  The hotel itself is wonderful.  The rooms are a good size and the bathroom amenities are superb with Aesop toiletries.  The staff made us feel as though we were honored guests and couldn't do enough for us.  Because we were there for 3 weeks we got to know quite a few.  So, when we needed to leave our home while it was being fumigated for 2 nights, we of course wanted to return to The Proper and our "friends."  Again, a wonderful experience.  The two restaurants, Palma and Calabra, are excellent.  We will definitely be going back to dine.  The fitness center has wonderful equipment and the Surya spa, which is on the property, is amazing.  I highly recommend this hotel if you are looking for a 5 star experience.

Good: Receptionist are very friendly, understanding & instant problem-solvers. Well done.    Superb Gym.    Bad: We got a room where we could hear the service door next to our room every few minutes, for 500 bucks a night?!         Plastic toilet seats with no softclosing lids. Just one shower outlet, very difficult to shower without washing hair.         Price performance foodwise disappoints.         Sauna/Cold Plunch is not included in the room fee, very unusual for a 5 star hotel. 150 dollar to rent for 1 person and extra 50 bucks to add another person. What is this?        Crossing paths when you step out of the elevator at ground floor and walking either to the lobby or gym. You really have to watch out to not bump into somebody. The staff is always in a rush and the corridors are very narrow.
Beautiful, resort like setting. Pool and roof deck are beautiful and service and food are top notch.

I've always enjoyed coming here in the warmer months. The rooftop has a very classy and fun atmosphere. The Mediterranean themed food is also great and the service at the hotel is excellent. I hope to be back soon.

I wasn't staying at the hotel, just having dinner at Palma in the lobby. My server, German, was outstanding!! He was so kind and thoughtful with his service and went above any beyond to deliver a truly exceptional experience. The food, wine and surprise dessert that delivered was wonderful! Highly recommend, and absolutely ask to sit in German's section when you dine at Palma!!
loved everything about this hotel, Its my favorite to date, ever and I travel a lot for work. Its beyond stunning, food is delicious, staff amazing!

My recent stay at the Proper Hotel in Santa Monica was nothing short of exceptional. The property itself is stunning—beautifully designed with a warm, relaxed atmosphere that perfectly fits the Santa Monica vibe.    A special shoutout to the rooftop team for making the experience unforgettable. Juan and Brandon, the rooftop managers, went above and beyond to make sure everything was perfect throughout our visit. Their professionalism and genuine hospitality really stood out. And Pablo, the bartender, deserves special mention—his cocktails were incredible, and his friendly, upbeat personality made every visit to the bar a highlight.    From start to finish, the service, ambiance, and attention to detail were top-notch. I can’t recommend the Proper Santa Monica enough and will definitely be returning soon.

My wife and I and stayed at the Proper for our wedding weekend in Oct ‘25. They superseded all expectations for the entire weekend. Above the beautiful spaces, great food, top notch service, the team you work with makes you feel right at home. They check on you regularly to make sure the experience is up to your standards. From valet to check in and the entire management team helped ensure we and all of our family/guests had a wonderful experience. The hospitality is out of this world.     Special thanks to Silvis for helping with bags and a mid-trip room change. Riley and Marcia at the front desk helped us settle in and address questions and kept the weekend going smoothly and Matt from the customer care team really went above and beyond to make our weekend feel extra special and reduced stress in other areas of the weekend. While the price may be a bit up there for many, you absolutely get all that value and more when staying at the Proper Santa Monica! Highly recommended!
We really loved our stay at Proper, it was an amazing location pool area and roof top bar was also perfect along with the food and drinks! Service was also great, we’ll definitely be back!!
The quality of the food was excellent, the setting lovely and the staff very welcoming and nice.
Took very long to take the order.
Long wait in the downstairs restaurant to order drinks and slow service
long wait for items, price does not meet food.
Bartender didn’t know how to make the drinks. Very slow service.
Strong suggestion: offer breakfast (which we were actually told is available) on rooftop! It would also eliminate apparent table availability downstairs
fantastic cocktails and mezze - great staff upstairs
Website says brunch was available and it was not; lunch food was good but menu seemed very limited

shout out for Brandon - friendly approach and helpful.  I dined alone and was made to feel particularly at home and comfortable and given a nice table - often lone diners can be shoved in a corner and forgotten about it - i do it a lot traveling for work. i appreciated the warm welcome and comfort of Calabra by Brandon and his team.
My brother and I had to sit in a back room. There was a bar there but we had to serve ourselves from the main bar. It was kind of a strange space.
It was preprepared menu based on our team meeting.  The Paella was amazing.
Having my table in the very loud bar was not favorable. It seems that all the tables were located there.

While the food and options were delicious at Calabra, the staff was fairly inattentive and we waited a very long time for an initial greeting. Once we had food, the server didn’t check back in for 20+ minutes, and the restaurant wasn’t very busy. Disappointing experience and given so many other great options for food in Santa Monica, would not recommend based on service.
Great location. Food had too many ingredients. Wish there were some simpler options.
wine wasn’t fabulous for price, dessert we ordered tasted frozen - odd, overall loved bread, food
We hit the hostess stand during shift change I guess so took a bit for new host to arrive.
I will come back just for that dungeness crab toast
The servers were really nice and capabale.
Seats/Chairs too low and not the most comfortable, too low. Makes for an uncomfortable seating experience.  Tables too low to eat breakfast in a comfortable manner
Service in restaurant was least hospitable of all service we received
Expensive for the portion sizes. $100+ for 2 Açaí Bowls, 2 cups of coffee, and 2 side orders of toast is a bit pricey.
Maybe more seating availability for breakfast
Need to improve gluten free  bread; there is much better available
see note about host desk. bar staff and waiters super friendly and efficient.
The breakfast menu was the best thing about the hotel
$21 for a titos and Club is quite expensive
I had a margarita and it was 31$ before tip. Very good but seems a bit much!
How can one be out of bacon?
French fries didn’t arrive with burger until later. Fries were broken small pieces.

the manager at the rooftop 4 helping us find seating and getting us drinks. He was exceptional.     The hostess on Friday night with brown hair, she was quite young. She was extremely friendly and she was from San Francisco at a time in her life. She got us seating, even though every single seat was taken because we were hotel guests. She was very friendly and made us very comfortable.
This restaurant is a gem.   The staff are the best!   Very professional.   Dinner was superb.  Love the vibe.
It took a long time to get a waiter to take our order in the mornings (like 20 minutes in one morning)
Glasses were not clean  Food was average
Promptly delivered by very nice staff
50 for a pot of coffee and a juice are silly (even in LA)!
My bagel was cold and not the one I ordered. I didn’t receive any cream with my coffee.
Estimate for delivery was 15-20 minutes but it took double that.
Phone call downstairs went unanswered twice. Also, the QR code doesn't allow me to pay on my room charge.
Incredible room service food quality and quick delivery of food!!
the salmon i ate was tasty. wish there were more options available for plant-based pescatarians.
Person who answered my call was friendly, patient, thorough and clear in answering my questions about preordering early AM delivery.
I wished there were more options, but the service was prompt and the food was good.
We only recieved one of the two smoothies  we ordered.  The quality of the food was ordinary.
excellent room service food quality and quite speedy fulfillment
the bill came out way larger than the listing price.  would be nice if room service charges were included in the final price.
The food at the restaurants was A+. In room dining was not nearly as good.
